question.label: Decide vs escalate
question.name: On decide versus escalate, what feels hardest to judge right now?
question.description: Drills into the specific boundary she named so the manager can remove ambiguity.

answer: Cross-team conversations move fast and I sometimes leave unsure about who committed to what.

assessment.note: She names a specific clarity gap in fast cross-team conversations: leaving unsure who committed to what.

new_queue.label: Thread follow
new_queue.name: ross-team conversations move — can you say more about what that means for you right now?

new_queue.label: Committed to what
new_queue.name: When that happens, what part gets lost — owners, decisions, or next steps?

new_queue.label: Alignment friction
new_queue.name: What part of running alignment is taking more effort than it should?

new_queue.label: Support from me
new_queue.name: Where would a clearer call from me help you most?

new_queue.label: Next support
new_queue.name: What would make the next two weeks feel steadier for you?

history.answer_excerpt: I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
I can draft plans, though I am not always sure when I should decide versus escalate.
Cross-team conversations move fast and I sometimes leave unsure about who committed to what.
